heading: Property loss: reimbursement of members for certain losses of household effects caused by hostile action
label: 2738 Property loss: reimbursement of members for certain losses of household effects caused by hostile action
notes: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 103–337, div. A, title V, § 557(c)108 Stat. 2776Oct. 5, 1994“(1), United States Code, as added by subsection (a), applies with respect to losses incurred after .Section 2738 of title 10June 30, 1990“(2)In the case of a loss incurred after , and before the date of the enactment of this Act [], a request for reimbursement shall be filed with the Secretary of the military department concerned not later than two years after such date of enactment.”June 30, 1990Oct. 5, 1994

children.heading: .—Authority To Reimburse
children.label: a .—Authority To Reimburse
children.text: The Secretary concerned may reimburse a member of the armed forces in an amount not more than $100,000 for a loss described in subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Covered Losses
children.label: b .—Covered Losses
children.text: This section applies with respect to a loss of household effects sustained during a move made incident to a change of permanent station when, as determined by the Secretary, the loss was caused by a hostile action incident to war or a warlike action by a military force.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: c .—Limitation
children.text: The Secretary may provide reimbursement under this section for a loss described in subsection (b) only to the extent that the loss is not reimbursed under insurance or under the authority of another provision of law.

children.heading: .—Applicability of Other Authorities and Requirements
children.label: d .—Applicability of Other Authorities and Requirements
children.text: Subsections (b), (d), (e), (f), and (g) of shall apply to a request for a reimbursement under this section as if the request were a claim against the United States.section 2733 of this title
